summary: Updated the pinned PrettyTable dependency in reqs/misc.txt from 3.11.0 to 3.14.0 so tools/easy_funcs.py works after a clean install with prettytable.TableStyle.PLAIN_COLUMNS. The PR notes that PrettyTable 3.11 lacked this syntax, while 3.14 supports it, and that the older non-TableStyle syntax is deprecated as of PrettyTable 3.12.0.
what_changed: Changed the PrettyTable requirement in reqs/misc.txt from prettytable==3.11.0 to prettytable==3.14.0.
Addressed a clean-install crash when running tools/easy_funcs.py caused by use of prettytable.TableStyle.PLAIN_COLUMNS on an older PrettyTable version.
Kept the scope deliberately small rather than updating the broader dependency set.
smart_moves: Fixed the tool breakage by updating the pinned dependency to a version that supports the syntax already used by the repository, instead of reverting to deprecated PrettyTable API usage.
Limited the PR to a one-line dependency bump, reducing risk in an old pinned requirements set.
Included rationale tied to upstream PrettyTable release behavior: non-TableStyle syntax is deprecated as of PrettyTable 3.12.0, while the repository needs TableStyle support.
decomp_lessons: When Python tooling adopts newer library APIs, verify that the pinned reqs files support those APIs on a clean install.
For decomp support scripts such as tools/easy_funcs.py, dependency pins are part of reproducibility; stale pins can break contributor onboarding even if local environments work.
Small dependency bumps can be preferable to broad requirements modernization when confidence in wider package interactions is low.
naming_conventions: PrettyTable's newer style API is referenced as prettytable.TableStyle.PLAIN_COLUMNS.
The affected generated requirements file is reqs/misc.txt, with the dependency sourced via reqs/misc.in according to the file comments.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.
The author commented with a screenshot and stated that it worked after the change.
The PR body flags a broader maintenance concern: many dependencies are old, but the author was not confident enough in the repo to update all of them in this PR.
follow_up_queries: Search for other reqs files pinning old Python dependencies that may be incompatible with current tool scripts.
Check tools/easy_funcs.py for PrettyTable TableStyle usage and confirm the minimum supported PrettyTable version.
Review whether reqs/misc.in should also be updated if reqs/misc.txt is generated from it.
Audit Python tooling dependency pins created around the same time as PrettyTable 3.11.0.
